trivia_bullets.text: Grasp of Darkness originated in Scars of Mirrodin (SOM, 2010) — a Phyrexian-corruption set where darkness was a literal contagion remaking the plane. The M21 printing is its fourth, following Oath of the Gatewatch (OGW, 2016) and Game Night 2019 (GN2). [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "I welcome the use of even the foulest magic if it will rid us of the monstrosities that plague our world." — Munda, ambush leader. Munda is a Kor warrior from Zendikar who served as a squad leader under Gideon Jura during the Battle for Zendikar and later the Oath of the Gatewatch events; he is represented by the legendary card Munda, Ambush Leader (BFZ). [Scryfall, WebSearch: Munda — mtg.wiki]

Grasp of Darkness is a Pauper-legal instant (BB, -4/-4) that has remained a consistent removal option across multiple formats since 2010. Its EDHREC rank of 10,726 reflects steady Commander inclusion as budget mono-black removal. [Scryfall]

The M21 printing features new art by Daarken, replacing Johann Bodin's original Scars of Mirrodin art, which carried the flavor line "On a world with five suns, night is compelled to become an aggressive force." The Munda flavor text is new to the M21 printing, recontextualizing the spell from Phyrexian horror to a Zendikari soldier's pragmatic willingness to use dark power. [Scryfall]
